title: relbunium croceum subsp. involucratum
intro: Relbunium croceum subsp. involucratum, also known as the hairy Relbunium or Relbunium involucratum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Relbunium croceum subsp. involucratum can be made from the plant's leaves and stems, often in the form of a salve or tincture, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
